disabled_touchpoints.description: List of touchpoint identifiers that are blocked from showing any offer screen. In ManageableOffersMapper.map(), if the incoming touchPoint matches any string in this array, the mapper returns CAN_NOT_OPEN immediately. Accepted values: any string matching a touchpoint name used by the app. Adding a touchpoint string here prevents the corresponding offer screen from ever appearing for that touchpoint.

disabled_subtouchpoints.description: List of sub-touchpoint identifiers that are blocked from showing any offer screen. If the incoming subTouchPoint matches any string in this array, the mapper returns CAN_NOT_OPEN. Accepted values: any string matching a sub-touchpoint name. Adding a sub-touchpoint here prevents the offer screen from appearing when that sub-touchpoint is active.

touchpoints.description: Array of touchpoint configuration objects defining which offer screens to show and under what conditions for each touchpoint. The mapper searches this array for a matching entry by name/names, falling back to one named "default" if no direct match is found. If no match exists or the matched entry has no info, the result is FAILURE and no offer is shown. Adding or removing entries controls which touchpoints have configured offers.

names.description: Alternative list of touchpoint identifiers that this configuration entry matches against. Checked via list containment if the single "name" field does not match the incoming touchpoint. Accepted values: array of touchpoint strings. Adding touchpoint strings here makes this single configuration entry apply to multiple touchpoints simultaneously.

name.description: Single touchpoint identifier used to match this configuration entry to an incoming touchpoint string via exact equality. Accepted values: any touchpoint string, or "default" for the fallback entry that is used when no other entry matches. Changing this determines which touchpoint triggers this configuration.

info.description: Array of offer parameter objects, each defining an offer screen configuration with optional launch-logic conditions. Entries are evaluated in order; the first entry whose launch_logic conditions pass is selected as the active offer. If empty or absent, the mapper returns FAILURE and no offer screen is shown. Adding entries provides prioritized offer screen options for this touchpoint.

thank_you_popup.description: Identifier for the thank-you popup shown after a successful purchase on this offer screen. Passed as thankYouPopupId to TransformableScreenParams. Accepted values: any string popup identifier recognized by the offer screen UI. Changing this controls which thank-you UI appears after the user completes a purchase.

launch_logic.description: Array of condition sets that gate whether this offer can be shown. Each entry is evaluated independently; if any single entry has all its conditions pass, the offer is eligible. If the array is empty or absent, the offer is shown unconditionally (always passes). Adding condition entries with stricter thresholds narrows the audience that sees this offer screen.

should_replace_with_ad.description: When true, the offer is only shown if an interstitial ad can be displayed (checked via canShowInterstitialAd). Combined with should_replace_with_reward_interstitial in the same gate check. Accepted values: true or false (null defaults to false). Setting to true gates this launch-logic entry on interstitial ad availability, blocking the offer if no ad is ready.

should_replace_with_reward_credit.description: When true, the offer is only shown if a credit-rewarded ad is available (checked via canShowRewardedAd with checkCreditsFlow=true). Accepted values: true or false (null defaults to false). Setting to true gates this launch-logic entry on credit-reward ad availability.

install_day_min.description: Minimum number of days since app install (inclusive) required for this launch logic to pass. Compared via isInRange against getPassedAppInstallDays(). Accepted values: integer >= 0, or null (defaults to -1 meaning no restriction; both min and max at -1 skips the check). Increasing this delays the offer until the user has had the app installed for more days.

editor_done_min.description: Minimum number of editor-done actions (inclusive) required for this launch logic to pass. Compared via isInRange against getEditorDoneCount(). Accepted values: integer >= 0, or null (defaults to -1 meaning no restriction). Increasing this delays the offer until the user has completed more editor export/save actions.

app_session_end.description: Upper bound (exclusive) on app session count for this launch logic to pass. When -1 and app_session_start >= 0, only the minimum is enforced (value >= start). Accepted values: integer > app_session_start, or null (defaults to -1 meaning no upper bound). Setting this caps the offer to only appear within a specific app session count range.

editor_done_max.description: Upper bound (exclusive) on editor-done action count. When -1 and editor_done_min >= 0, only the minimum is enforced. Accepted values: integer > editor_done_min, or null (defaults to -1 meaning no upper bound). Setting this limits the offer to users within a specific editor-done count window.

session_limit_start.description: Minimum number of in-memory per-touchpoint offer views (inclusive) required. Tracked in an in-memory map keyed by touchpoint + screens, reset on app restart. Accepted values: integer >= 0, or null (defaults to -1 meaning no restriction). Increasing this means the offer only shows after being triggered a certain number of times within the current app process.

had_subscription_package.description: List of subscription package IDs; the check passes if the user's purchase history includes a past subscription for any listed package (checked via userHadSubscriptionForPackage). Accepted values: array of package ID strings (e.g., Google Play product IDs). When empty or null, the check is skipped. Adding package IDs restricts the offer to users who previously subscribed to specific packages.

user_had_subscription.description: Filters by whether the user has ever had any subscription. When null, the check is skipped entirely. When true, only users with past subscriptions see the offer; when false, only users who never subscribed. Accepted values: true, false, or null. Changing this targets the offer to different subscription history segments.

should_replace_with_reward_interstitial.description: When true, the offer is only shown if an interstitial ad can be shown with skipMinShowIntervalCheck=true. Combined with should_replace_with_ad in the same gate check in checkAdsFlows(). Accepted values: true or false (null defaults to false). Setting to true gates this entry on reward-interstitial ad availability, bypassing the minimum show interval.

key_session_start.description: Minimum number of persistent key-session views (inclusive) required, tracked per the parent's session_key in SharedPreferences. Only effective when session_key is non-empty. Accepted values: integer >= 0, or null (defaults to -1 meaning no restriction). Increasing this delays the offer until the user has triggered it a certain number of times for this session_key across app restarts.

install_day_max.description: Upper bound (exclusive) on days since app install. When -1 and install_day_min >= 0, only the minimum is enforced. Accepted values: integer > install_day_min, or null (defaults to -1 meaning no upper bound). Setting this restricts the offer to users within a specific install-age window (e.g., first 7 days).

is_ai_credit.description: Filters by whether the current user context is an AI credit flow (checked via creditsTransactionUseCase.isCreditFlow()). When null, the check is skipped. When true, the offer only shows during credit flow; when false, only outside credit flow. Accepted values: true, false, or null. Changing this targets the offer to credit or non-credit usage contexts.

offer_screen_touchpoint.description: Preferred field for overriding the analytics touchpoint at the launch-logic level; takes priority over the legacy offer_screen_touch_point field. Mapped to OfferLaunchLogicParams.offerScreenTouchPoint. Accepted values: any touchpoint string. Changing this updates the analytics touchpoint reported for this specific launch-logic condition set.

repeat_key_session.description: Key-session repeat interval controlling periodic re-showing of the offer based on the persistent key-session counter. When set and session_key is non-empty, the offer shows when (keySessionCount + 1) % (repeatKeySession + 1) == 0. Accepted values: integer >= 0, or null (defaults to -1 meaning no repeat constraint). Setting to e.g. 2 shows the offer every 3rd key-session trigger.

storage_min_percent.description: Minimum device storage usage percentage (inclusive) for this launch logic to pass. Compared via isInRange against getCachedStorageUsagePercent(). Accepted values: integer 0-100, or null (defaults to -1 meaning no restriction). Setting this targets the offer to users whose cloud storage usage is above a certain threshold.

is_store_available.description: Filters by whether the Google Play Store is available as an active payment method (checked via getActivePaymentTypes().contains(PaymentServiceType.Google)). Accepted values: true or false (null defaults to true). When true, the offer only shows if Google Play is active; when false, only if it is not. Changing this targets the offer based on payment service availability.

key_session_end.description: Upper bound (exclusive) on persistent key-session views. Only effective when session_key is non-empty. When -1 and key_session_start >= 0, only the minimum is enforced. Accepted values: integer > key_session_start, or null (defaults to -1 meaning no upper bound). Setting this caps how many times the offer can appear for a given session_key.

repeat_session.description: App session repeat interval. When set, the offer only shows when appSessionCount % repeatSession == 0. Accepted values: integer > 0, or null (defaults to -1 meaning no repeat constraint). Setting to e.g. 3 shows the offer only on every 3rd app session.

offer_screen_touch_point.description: Legacy field for overriding the analytics touchpoint at the launch-logic level. Superseded by offer_screen_touchpoint; only used when offer_screen_touchpoint is null. Accepted values: any touchpoint string. Changing this alters the analytics touchpoint for this launch-logic entry when the newer field is absent.

restricted_packages.description: List of package/offer ID pairs used to check user eligibility for promotional offers. All entries must pass isUserEligibleForPromotionalOffer(packageId, offerId) for the launch logic to succeed. When null or empty, the check is skipped. Adding entries restricts the offer to users eligible for all specified promotional packages.

package_id.description: The subscription package identifier passed to isUserEligibleForPromotionalOffer() as the first argument. Accepted values: any Google Play subscription product ID string (null defaults to empty string). Changing this alters which subscription package is checked for promotional eligibility.

offer_id.description: The promotional offer identifier passed to isUserEligibleForPromotionalOffer() alongside package_id. Accepted values: any Google Play promotional offer ID string (null defaults to empty string). Changing this alters which specific promotional offer eligibility is verified.

current_tier_types.description: List of subscription tier types the user must currently hold for this offer to show. The code checks if the user's current tierType.value is in this list; the special value "free" matches non-subscribed users. When empty or null, the check is skipped (all tiers match). Accepted values: tier type strings such as "free" or other tier identifiers. Adding values restricts the offer to users on specific subscription tiers.

app_session_start.description: Minimum number of app sessions (inclusive) required for this launch logic to pass. Compared via isInRange against getAppSessionCount(). Accepted values: integer >= 0, or null (defaults to -1 meaning no restriction; both start and end at -1 skips the check). Increasing this value delays the offer until the user has opened the app more times.

daily_session_limit.description: Maximum number of times this offer can be shown per calendar day for a given touchpoint. Tracked in SharedPreferences with a 24-hour reset window. Accepted values: integer >= 0, or null (defaults to -1 meaning unlimited daily shows). Decreasing this value reduces how frequently users see this offer each day.

should_replace_with_reward.description: When true, the offer is only shown if a rewarded ad is available for the current touchpoint (checked via canShowRewardedAd). If no rewarded ad is available, this launch-logic entry fails. Accepted values: true or false (null defaults to false). Setting to true gates this entry on rewarded ad availability.

skip_offer_during_cooldown.description: When true, this offer is suppressed during the one-day-pass cooldown period (default 30 days after a one-day-pass grant expires, configurable via one_day_pass_config). The launch logic only passes if isCooldownPeriodPassed() returns true. Accepted values: true or false (null defaults to false). Setting to true prevents the offer from appearing while the user is in a post-one-day-pass cooldown window.

session_limit_end.description: Upper bound (exclusive) on in-memory per-touchpoint offer views. When -1 and session_limit_start >= 0, only the minimum is enforced. Accepted values: integer > session_limit_start, or null (defaults to -1 meaning no upper bound). Setting this limits how many times per app process lifetime the offer can appear for this touchpoint.

storage_max_percent.description: Upper bound (exclusive) on device storage usage percentage. When -1 and storage_min_percent >= 0, only the minimum is enforced. Accepted values: integer > storage_min_percent up to 100, or null (defaults to -1 meaning no upper bound). Setting this restricts the offer to users within a specific storage usage range.

offer_variant.description: A freeform string identifier passed to analytics as offerVariant and to the offer screen UI as contentVariant, used for A/B testing and variant tracking. Accepted values: any string. Changing this updates the variant label reported in analytics events and may influence which offer screen content variant is rendered.

screens.description: Ordered list of offer screen identifiers that the app will display. The first entry determines the initial screen shown (mapped to SubscriptionFullScreenName via subscriptionFullScreenNameByKey). Subsequent entries are queued via OfferScreensManager. Also used to build a composite key for session counting (touchpoint + screens joined by dot). Accepted values: screen name strings recognized by OfferScreensManager (e.g., "transformable", offer screen keys). Changing this controls which subscription/offer UI the user sees.

offer_screen_touchpoint.description: Preferred field for overriding the analytics touchpoint for this offer at the info level; takes priority over the legacy offer_screen_touch_point field. Mapped to OfferScreenParam.offerScreenTouchPoint. Accepted values: any touchpoint string. Changing this updates the analytics touchpoint and may affect screen-name resolution for this offer.

session_key.description: A unique string key used to persist and track a per-offer session counter across app restarts (stored in SharedPreferences). When non-empty, the app increments and reads this key to enforce key_session_start/key_session_end and repeat_key_session limits in launch_logic. Accepted values: any non-empty string (empty or null disables key-session tracking). Changing this value effectively resets the counter since a new preference key is used.

is_one_time_offer.description: Flags whether this offer is a one-time (non-subscription) purchase. When true, the app checks for pending one-time purchases and may show a pending-purchase popup instead of the offer screen. Also skips the 'cannot upgrade from other platform' popup that normally blocks subscription offers. Accepted values: true or false (null defaults to false). Setting to true enables one-time purchase flow handling for this offer entry.

offer_screen_touch_point.description: Legacy field for overriding the analytics touchpoint for this offer. Superseded by offer_screen_touchpoint; only used when offer_screen_touchpoint is null. Accepted values: any touchpoint string. Changing this alters the analytics touchpoint reported for this offer when the newer field is absent.

all_files.role: Determines whether a manageable offer screen should be shown based on launch logic, limits, subscription state, and ad configuration.
Interface defining getOfferName and incrementCounts operations for manageable offer screen resolution.
Interface defining offer screen resolution by touch point, session counting, and app session tracking.
Fetches OfferShowLogicApiModel from remote settings, delegates mapping to ManageableOffersMapper, and manages session/daily counters.
Maps OfferShowLogicApiModel API response into OfferScreenLaunchEntity domain model with launch logic params.
@FeatureStart API model deserialized from remote settings key offer_screen_show_logic_sf containing touchpoint offer configurations.
Domain entity file containing OfferScreenParam, OfferScreenLaunchEntity, OfferScreenParamEntity, OfferScreenState, OfferLaunchLogicParams, and RestrictedPackagesEntity.
Input parameter data class carrying touchPoint, forceShow flag, and tool usage info for offer resolution.
Sealed interface defining payment service types (Google, Fake, Web) used in Play Store availability checks.
Provides cached storage usage percentage used in storage-based offer launch logic range checks.
Provides user subscription state, purchase history, active payment types, and package details.
Tracks whether a fullscreen offer is currently showing to prevent concurrent offer displays.
Checks interstitial and rewarded ad availability for ad-replacement offer logic flows.
Provides one-day pass cooldown period status used to skip offers during cooldown.
Determines if the user is in a credit-based flow to conditionally filter offers flagged as credit-only.
Awaits ongoing PA guard subscription access requests before resolving offer eligibility.
Checks user eligibility for promotional offers used in restricted packages validation.
Data class representing purchase history entries used in had-subscription eligibility checks.
Entity carrying cooldown period and visual clue text for one-day pass configuration.
Entity defining cooldown length and without-trial days count for one-day pass period calculations.

DI module binding SubscriptionRepo and SubscriptionInfoUseCase implementations.
DI module binding SubscriptionAccessRepo implementation.
DI module binding OneDayPassConfigRepo implementation.
Delegates interstitial and rewarded ad availability checks to InterstitialService and RewardedAdService.
Reads one-day pass config from RemoteSettings, delegates mapping to OneDayPassConfigMapper, checks cooldown via SubscriptionRepo.

API model for one-day pass configuration deserialized from remote settings.
Implements PA guard subscription access via SubscriptionApiService, manages concurrent request deduplication with Mutex.
Retrofit API service providing getSettingsWithTag(), provideSubscriptionAccess(), and requestPAUserSubscriptionGranted() endpoints.
The only direct consumer of SubscriptionManageableOffersUseCase — calls getOfferName() with ManageableFullScreenPromotionParam and posts OfferScreenParamEntity to LiveData.
Observes SubscriptionFullScreenNavigationViewModel LiveData to receive resolved OfferScreenParamEntity and drives actual offer screen navigation.

tree.role: Observes SubscriptionFullScreenNavigationViewModel LiveData to receive resolved OfferScreenParamEntity and drives actual offer screen navigation.

children.role: The only direct consumer of SubscriptionManageableOffersUseCase — calls getOfferName() with ManageableFullScreenPromotionParam and posts OfferScreenParamEntity to LiveData.
Determines whether a manageable offer screen should be shown based on launch logic, limits, subscription state, and ad configuration.
Interface defining offer screen resolution by touch point, session counting, and app session tracking.
Fetches OfferShowLogicApiModel from remote settings, delegates mapping to ManageableOffersMapper, and manages session/daily counters via preferences.
Maps OfferShowLogicApiModel API response into OfferScreenLaunchEntity domain model, converting touch point details and launch logic params.
@FeatureStart API model deserialized from remote settings key offer_screen_show_logic_sf, containing disabled touchpoints and per-touchpoint offer configurations.
Retrofit API service providing getSettingsWithTag() for tag-based remote settings fetch used by SubscriptionManageableOffersRepoImpl.
Provides cached storage usage percentage used to evaluate storage-based offer launch logic range checks.
Provides user subscription state, purchase history, active payment types, and package details used in multiple offer eligibility checks.
Tracks whether a fullscreen offer is currently showing to prevent concurrent offer displays.
Checks interstitial and rewarded ad availability for ad-replacement offer logic flows.
Delegates interstitial and rewarded ad availability checks to InterstitialService and RewardedAdService.
Provides one-day pass cooldown period status used to skip offers during cooldown.
Reads one-day pass config from RemoteSettings, delegates mapping to OneDayPassConfigMapper, checks cooldown via SubscriptionRepo.
Maps OneDayPassConfigApiModel to OneDayPassConfigEntity domain model.
API model for one-day pass configuration deserialized from remote settings.
Determines if the user is in a credit-based flow to conditionally filter offers flagged as credit-only.
Awaits ongoing PA guard subscription access requests before resolving offer eligibility.
Implements PA guard subscription access via SubscriptionApiService, manages concurrent request deduplication with Mutex.
